fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Idaho Speech-Language Pathologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Idaho - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: speech-language pathologist license reciprocity idaho, how to transfer speech-language pathologist license to idaho, speech-language pathologist compact idaho, idaho speech-language pathologist endorsement application

board.name: Bureau of Occupational Licenses — Speech and Hearing Services

license_types.title: Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist
license_types.description: The full credential to independently practice speech-language pathology in Idaho.

license_types.title: Provisional SLP License
license_types.description: Issued to SLP graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.

steps.title: Earn a Master's Degree in SLP
steps.description: Complete a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

steps.title: Pass the Praxis Examination
steps.description: Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination (test code 5331, score 162).

steps.title: Complete Clinical Fellowship
steps.description: Complete 36 weeks of supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours).

steps.title: Submit Application
steps.description: Submit application with $75 fee to Bureau of Occupational Licenses.

documents.detail: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

telepractice_text: Idaho permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact.
provisional_text: Idaho issues a Provisional SLP License for graduates completing their Clinical Fellowship.
reciprocity_text: Idaho is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Idaho through a compact privilege.

faqs.question: How much does an Idaho SLP license cost?
faqs.answer: The application and license fee is $75. Idaho has among the lowest SLP licensing fees. Annual renewal is $75.

faqs.question: Is Idaho part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?
faqs.answer: Yes. Idaho has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

faqs.question: Does Idaho allow telepractice for SLPs?
faqs.answer: Yes. Idaho permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

faqs.question: Does Idaho have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Idaho participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Idaho without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Idaho?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Idaho while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Idaho is a member of the ASLP Interstate Compact. SLPs from other compact member states can practice in Idaho through a compact privilege.
